member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.InvalidHandleValueException
member.summary: Invalid descriptor value

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.FreeLoadOptionIndexNotFound
member.summary: Failed to find free loadOption index

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.InvalidInheritedClassException
member.summary: The registering type has the wrong parent

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.DeviceProtocolCastingException
member.summary: Failed to cast DevicePathProtocol

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.InvalidLoadOptionStrcutreException
member.summary: Load option has incorrect structure

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.MissingDevicePathProtocolWrapperAttributeException
member.summary: The wrapper class does not contain a defining attribute

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.InvalidDevicePathProtocolStructureException
member.summary: The wrapper class has the wrong structure

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService
member.summary: Provides methods to read, write, update, and delete boot entries from your computer's UEFI NVRAM

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.CurrentLoadOptionIndex
member.summary: Gets the index of the boot record this computer was booted from. Use this index in methods of this

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.NextLoadOptionIndex
member.summary: Sets the index of the boot entry that will be loaded next time ONCE. Use this index in methods of this class

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.LoadOrder
member.summary: Gets or sets the boot order. The array contains the indexes of all boot entries in the order in which they will be loaded. Use this index in methods of this class

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.DeleteLoadOption(System.Firmware.BootOptionIndex)
member.summary: Resets the Boot#### variable at the specified index and removes it from the boot order

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.EnumerateBootOptions
member.summary: Lists all load options in boot order

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.EnumrateBootOptions``1
member.summary: Lists all load options in boot order

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.EnumrateRawBootOptions
member.summary: Lists all load options in boot order

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.EditLoadOption(System.Firmware.BootOptionIndex,System.Action{System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.FirmwareBootOption})
member.summary: Reads the boot option from NVRAM, issues it to the delegate for processing and updates the variable at the specified index

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.CreateLoadOption(System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionBase,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new load option at the first free index, writes into it a serialized copy of the instance passed to the function, and returns the index of the new entry. Specify the parameter as to add the option as the first boot option

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.UpdateLoadOption(System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionBase,System.Firmware.BootOptionIndex)
member.summary: Writes serialized copy of the instance passed to the function into existing load option variable at the specified index

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.ReadRawLoadOption(System.Firmware.BootOptionIndex)
member.summary: Reads the native representation of the boot option from NVRAM

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.ReadLoadOption(System.Firmware.BootOptionIndex)
member.summary: Reads boot option from NVRAM

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.FirmwareBootService.ReadLoadOption``1(System.Firmware.BootOptionIndex)
member.summary: Reads a boot option from NVRAM and converts it to the specified type

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.FirmwareBootOption
member.summary: Basic implementation of

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.FirmwareBootOption.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the FirmwareBootOption class with default attribute and description values.
member.remarks: The Attributes property is set to LoadOptionAttributes.CATEGORY_BOOT, and the Description property is initialized as an empty string. This constructor is typically used when creating a boot option with default settings before customizing its properties.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.FirmwareBootOption.#ctor(System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionAttributes,System.String)
member.summary: Create new load option instance from and Option description

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.FirmwareBootOption.#ctor(System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionAttributes,System.String,System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DevicePathProtocolBase[],System.Byte[])
member.summary: Create new load option instance from , Option description, and s

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionAttributes
member.summary: The attributes for this load option entry

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionAttributes.ACTIVE
member.summary: If a load option is marked as LOAD_OPTION_ACTIVE, the boot manager will attempt to boot automatically using the device path information in the load option

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionAttributes.FORCE_RECONNECT
member.summary: If any Driver#### load option is marked as LOAD_OPTION_FORCE_RECONNECT , then all of the UEFI drivers in the system will be disconnected and reconnected after the last Driver#### load option is processed

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionAttributes.HIDDEN
member.summary: If any Boot#### load option is marked as LOAD_OPTION_HIDDEN , then the load option will not appear in the menu (if any) provided by the boot manager for load option selection

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionAttributes.CATEGORY
member.summary: The LOAD_OPTION_CATEGORY is a sub-field of Attributes that provides details to the boot manager to describe how it should group the Boot#### load options

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionAttributes.CATEGORY_BOOT
member.summary: Boot#### load options with LOAD_OPTION_CATEGORY set to LOAD_OPTION_CATEGORY_BOOT are meant to be part of the normal boot processing

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionAttributes.CATEGORY_APP
member.summary: Boot#### load options with LOAD_OPTION_CATEGORY set to LOAD_OPTION_CATEGORY_APP are executables which are not part of the normal boot processing but can be optionally chosen for execution if boot menu is provided, or via Hot Keys

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionBase
member.summary: Load option used to determine the boot parameters of a specific object, be it a driver or an OS

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionBase.#ctor
member.summary: Load option used to determine the boot parameters of a specific object, be it a driver or an OS

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionBase.Attributes
member.summary: The attributes for this load option entry

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionBase.Description
member.summary: The user readable description for the load option

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionBase.Protocols
member.summary: FilePathList A packed array of UEFI device paths.The first element of the array is a device path that describes the device and location of the Image for this load option.The FilePathList[0] is specific to the device type.Other device paths may optionally exist in the FilePathList, but their usage is OSV specific. Each element in the array is variable length, and ends at the device path end structure. Because the size of Description is arbitrary, this data structure is not guaranteed to be aligned on a natural boundary.This data structure may have to be copied to an aligned natural boundary before it is used

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionBase.OptionalData
member.summary: The remaining bytes in the load option descriptor are a binary data buffer that is passed to the loaded image. If the field is zero bytes long, a NULL pointer is passed to the loaded image. The number of bytes in OptionalData can be computed by subtracting the starting offset of OptionalData from total size in bytes of the EFI_LOAD_OPTION

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DefineDevicePathProtocolAttribute
member.summary: Attribute describing the wrapper class for the DevicePath protocol

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DefineDevicePathProtocolAttribute.#ctor(System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DeviceProtocolType,System.Byte)
member.summary: Attribute describing the wrapper class for the DevicePath protocol

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DefineDevicePathProtocolAttribute.Type
member.summary: Type of protocol to be wrapped

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DefineDevicePathProtocolAttribute.SubType
member.summary: SubType of protocol to be wrapped

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DevicePathProtocolBase
member.summary: A Device Path is used to define the programmatic path to a device. The primary purpose of a Device Path is to allow an application, such as an OS loader, to determine the physical device that the interfaces are abstracting.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DevicePathProtocolBase.#ctor(System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DeviceProtocolType,System.Byte)
member.summary: A Device Path is used to define the programmatic path to a device. The primary purpose of a Device Path is to allow an application, such as an OS loader, to determine the physical device that the interfaces are abstracting.

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DevicePathProtocolBase.Type
member.summary: Protocol type

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DevicePathProtocolBase.SubType
member.summary: Protocol Sub-Type - Varies by Type

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DevicePathProtocolBase.GetSerializationDataLength
member.summary: Returns the length, in bytes, of the data required to serialize the current object.
member.remarks: Implementations should ensure that the returned length accurately reflects the size of the serialized data for the current object state. This value can be used to allocate buffers or validate storage requirements before serialization.
member.returns: The number of bytes needed to serialize the object. The value is always non-negative.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DevicePathProtocolBase.Deserialize(System.IO.BinaryReader,System.UInt16)
member.summary: Deserialize raw data into managed data.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DevicePathProtocolBase.Serialize(System.IO.BinaryWriter)
member.summary: Serialize managed data into raw data

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DeviceProtocolType
member.summary: Type of device path protocol

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DeviceProtocolType.Hardware
member.summary: Hardware Device Path

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DeviceProtocolType.ACPI
member.summary: ACPI Device Path

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DeviceProtocolType.Message
member.summary: Messaging Device Path

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DeviceProtocolType.Media
member.summary: Media Device Path

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DeviceProtocolType.BIOS
member.summary: BIOS Boot Specification Device Path

member.name: F:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DeviceProtocolType.End
member.summary: End of Hardware Device Path

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DevicePathProtocolEnd
member.summary: This protocol is the final protocol for any boot option; if it is not specified, the option will be considered invalid

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DevicePathProtocolEnd.#ctor
member.summary: This protocol is the final protocol for any boot option; if it is not specified, the option will be considered invalid

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.RawMediaDevicePath
member.summary: The default instance for protocols, has no abstractions, but only raw data

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.RawMediaDevicePath.#ctor(System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DeviceProtocolType,System.Byte)
member.summary: The default instance for protocols, has no abstractions, but only raw data

summary.para: GUID - An EFI GUID in standard format xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx.
Keyword - In some cases, one of a series of keywords must be listed.
Integer - Unless otherwise specified, this indicates an unsigned integer in the range of 0 to 2^32-1. The value is decimal, unless preceded by �0x� or �0X�, in which case it is hexadecimal.

EISAID - A seven character text identifier in the format used by the ACPI specification. The first three characters must be alphabetic, either upper or lower case. The second four characters are hexadecimal digits, either numeric, upper case or lower case. Optionally, it can be the number 0.
String - Series of alphabetic, numeric and punctuation characters not including a right parenthesis �)�, bar �
HexDump - Series of bytes, represented by two hexadecimal characters per byte. Unless otherwise indicated, the size is only limited by the length of the device node.

IPv4 Address - Series of four integer values (each between 0-255), separated by a �.� Optionally, followed by a �:� and an integer value between 0-65555. If the �:� is not present, then the port value is zero.

IPv6 Address - IPv6 Address is expressed in the format [address]:port. The �address� is expressed in the way defined in RFC4291 Section 2.2. The �:port� after the [address] is optional. If present, the �port� is an integer value between 0-65535 to represent the port number, or else, port number is zero.

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.DevicePathProtocolWrapperSelector
member.summary: An assistant class that tells the marshaler what type to use to represent the native structure

member.name: P:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.DevicePathProtocolWrapperSelector.DisallowCastingUnknownWrappersToRawProtocol
member.summary: Tells if the marshaler should throw an exception when it encounters a protocol for which no wrapper is registered, instead of just returning a instance.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.DevicePathProtocolWrapperSelector.GetRegisteredType(System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DeviceProtocolType,System.Byte)
member.summary: Retrieves the registered protocol wrapper type associated with the specified protocol type and subtype.
member.remarks: This method returns only types that have been previously registered using RegisterWrapperLibrary. If no matching type is found, the method returns null.
member.param: The protocol type for which to retrieve the registered wrapper type.
The protocol subtype used to further identify the wrapper type. Typically represents a protocol variant or extension.
member.returns: The registered wrapper type corresponding to the specified protocol type and subtype, or null if no matching type is registered.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.DevicePathProtocolWrapperSelector.RegisterType``1
member.summary: Registers a protocol wrapper type for later use in marshaling boot options

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.DevicePathProtocolWrapperSelector.RegisterType(System.Type)
member.summary: Registers a protocol wrapper type for later use in marshaling boot options

member.name: T:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for marshalling UEFI boot service structures.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.ReadRawLoadOption(System.IO.BinaryReader)
member.summary: Reads an EFI load option from the binary reader without processing device path protocols into specific types.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.ReadRawDevicePathProtocol(System.IO.BinaryReader)
member.summary: Reads a raw EFI device path protocol from the binary reader.
member.returns: The raw EFI device path protocol.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.ReadLoadOption(System.IO.BinaryReader)
member.summary: Reads a firmware boot option from the binary reader.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.ReadLoadOption(System.IO.BinaryReader,System.Type)
member.summary: Reads a load option of the specified type from the binary reader.
member.param: The type of the load option to read.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.ReadDevicePathProtocol(System.IO.BinaryReader)
member.summary: Reads a device path protocol from the binary reader, automatically determining the specific protocol type.
member.exception: Thrown when the protocol data length does not match the expected length.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.ReadDevicePathProtocol(System.IO.BinaryReader,System.Type)
member.summary: Reads a device path protocol of the specified type from the binary reader.
member.param: The type of the device path protocol.
member.exception: Thrown when the protocol type or subtype does not match the expected type.
Thrown when the protocol data length does not match the expected length.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.ReadLoadOption``1(System.IO.BinaryReader)
member.summary: Reads a load option of type T from the binary reader.
member.typeparam: The type of the load option.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.ReadDevicePathProtocol``1(System.IO.BinaryReader)
member.summary: Reads a device path protocol of type T from the binary reader.
member.typeparam: The type of the device path protocol.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.WriteLoadOption(System.IO.BinaryWriter,System.Firmware.BootService.UefiNative.EFI_LOAD_OPTION)
member.summary: Writes a raw EFI load option to the binary writer.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.WriteDevicePathProtocol(System.IO.BinaryWriter,System.Firmware.BootService.UefiNative.EFI_DEVICE_PATH_PROTOCOL)
member.summary: Writes a raw EFI device path protocol to the binary writer.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.WriteLoadOption``1(System.IO.BinaryWriter,``0)
member.summary: Writes a load option to the binary writer.
member.typeparam: The type of the load option.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.WriteDevicePathProtocol``1(System.IO.BinaryWriter,``0)
member.summary: Writes a device path protocol to the binary writer.
member.typeparam: The type of the device path protocol.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.GetStrcutureLength(System.Firmware.BootService.LoadOption.LoadOptionBase)
member.summary: Calculates the total structure length of the load option in bytes.

member.name: M:System.Firmware.BootService.Win32Native.MarshalExtensions.GetStrctureLength(System.Firmware.BootService.Protocols.DevicePathProtocolBase)
member.summary: Calculates the total structure length of the device path protocol in bytes.
